ccel.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

title: To the Salonitans.

source.title: NPNF-212. Leo the Great, Gregory the Great

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Gregory to his most beloved sons, the clergy and nobles dwelling at Salona.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: It has come to my ears, that certain men of perverse disposition, in order to poison your minds, beloved, have tried to insinuate to you that I am moved by some grudge against Maximus, and that I am desiring to carry out not so much what is canonical as what anger dictates.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But far, far be it from the priestly mind to be moved in any cause by private feeling.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: It is on the contrary as taking thought for you, beloved, and as fearing the judgment of Almighty God on my own soul, that I desire the case of this same Maximus to be thoroughly investigated, as to whether he is burdened by no such crimes as are a bar to ordination, and makes no attempt to attain to the priestly office through simoniacal heresy; that is by giving bribes to some of his electors.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: He will then be a free intercessor for you before the Lord, if he shall come to the place of intercession bound by no sins of his own.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And yet his sin of pride is already manifestly shewn, in that, having been summoned to come to us, he resists under various excuses, shuns coming, is afraid to come.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: What then is he afraid of, if his conscience does not accuse him with respect to the things he is charged with?

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Lo, beloved, ye have now been long without a pastor, and may Almighty God make known to you how earnestly and from the bottom of my heart I sympathize with you in your destitution.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For I hear what ravages are being made in the Lord’s flock.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, when there is no shepherd, who may watch against the wolves?

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Wherefore urge ye the aforesaid Maximus to come hither to us, to the end that we may confirm him if we are able to find him innocent; but, if the things that are said of him should turn out to be true, that you, beloved, may be no longer left destitute through the interposition of his person.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For as to me, be assured that I am not moved against him by any grudge or any animosity of private feeling; but whatever may be canonical and just with the help of God I will determine.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But I have been greatly astonished that among so many clergy and people of the Church of Salona hardly two in sacred orders have been found—to wit our brother and fellow-bishop Paulinus and my most beloved son Honoratus, archdeacon of the same Church—who refused to communicate with Maximus when he seized the priesthood, and who remembered that they were Christians.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: For you ought, most dear sons, to have considered your own orders, and recognized as rejected him whom the Apostolical See rejected, that he might first be purged, if he could be, from the charges brought against him, and that then your Love might communicate with him without being partakers in his liability.

verses.book: Register of the Epistles of St. Gregory the Great.
verses.text: We however are bound to your Charity in the bowels of loving-kindness; and, since we have learnt that some of you were pressed by force to accept him and communicate with him, we implore Almighty God to absolve you from all guilt of your own sins and from all implication in the liability of others, and to give you the grace of His protection in the present life, and grant to us to rejoice for you in the eternal country.
